signals.description: Users who have been using the product for years, often since earlier versions or under a different name, and remain highly satisfied.
evidence: Been using it for years, even back when it was MacIntax. Best tax product available in my view.
Been using it for years, even back when it was MacIntax. Best tax product available in my view.

signals.description: Users express concern or dissatisfaction with price increases, especially when on a fixed income, or feel misled by hidden fees.
evidence: The price has doubled though since I first started using this product and as a senior on a fixed income that is what I don't like.
The pricing is a scam, after you purchase you find state, that you thought was included, actually has a fee to e-file.

signals.description: Users highlight the product's ease of use, installation, and user-friendly interface as key strengths.
evidence: Love how easy it is to adjust the settings.
Great product, easy to install, excellent price.
The program is easy to use.
I was up and running in about 5 minutes.

signals.description: Users report that a fundamental, advertised feature of the product is completely broken or non-functional.
evidence: The Insert Graphics feature in V3 does not work. An error is generated every time.
Defective disc, gets get stuck over and over trying to load in the drive...

signals.description: Users experience problems with receiving or installing updates, leading to version disparities and missing features.
evidence: He has version 5.1 and I have 3.1. MAKE SURE YOU ARE BUYING 5.1! I tried to find an update or way to get an update to no avail.
I tried doing it from inside the app, from the application in system settings, and from the website.

signals.description: Users note significant differences in features, versions, or user experience between different operating systems (e.g., iOS vs. Android).
evidence: He has version 5.1 and I have 3.1.
He got a page of sounds that I didn't. And had a really cool clock that I just found out about now.

signals.description: Users are unhappy with the customer support, citing unhelpful responses, lack of resolution, or feeling unsupported.
evidence: Nova is not supporting the product.
Nova's response was 'would you like to trade it in for a different Nova software package?'

signals.description: Users express enthusiasm for the core idea or purpose of the product, even if they note specific issues.
evidence: Great app concept. Anyone working on a computer a lot can benefit from the organization.
Really looks like an awesome app

signals.description: Users specifically compliment the graphics, visual selections, and overall aesthetic of the product.
evidence: Excellent graphics! Beautiful selections
Excellent graphics! Beautiful selections

signals.description: Users report a specific, physical defect in the product (e.g., defective disc) that prevents basic operation.
evidence: Defective disc, gets get stuck over and over trying to load in the drive...
I've never had any other disc ever get stuck in laptop disc drive before, and others discs still go in just fine, so its definitely a defective product

signals.description: Users explicitly state that the product is the best available option in its category.
evidence: Best tax product available in my view.

signals.description: Users describe how the product affects their ability to get work done, either positively or negatively.
evidence: Instead of spending my time productively now most of my energy is wasted on finding ways to trick my computer into letting me do what I want to do
Anyone working on a computer a lot can benefit from the organization.

signals.description: Users comment on whether the product's price is justified by its features, performance, or quality.
evidence: Great product, easy to install, excellent price.
The price has doubled though since I first started using this product and as a senior on a fixed income that is what I don't like.

signals.description: Users describe the process of installing the product, highlighting ease or difficulty.
evidence: Great product, easy to install
I was up and running in about 5 minutes.

signals.description: Users note differences in features or performance between versions of the same product.
evidence: He has version 5.1 and I have 3.1.
He got a page of sounds that I didn't.

signals.description: Users describe missing features or disparities that imply a desire for parity or additional functionality.
evidence: He got a page of sounds that I didn't. And had a really cool clock that I just found out about now.
I only need the basic edition and can't seem to find it anywhere locally.

signals.description: Users express personal feelings, encouragement, or relate the product to their own experiences.
evidence: This post made my day, as I was overthinking to work on my own.
I am about to release my first OS project aswell and I am shaky
Hoping that everything will go smooth for you, and all the very best ;)

signals.description: Users express frustration with being forced to use specific programs or features against their will, indicating a loss of control.
evidence: Instead of spending my time productively now most of my energy is wasted on finding ways to trick my computer into letting me do what I want to do, not the default programs windows wants you to use.

signals.description: Users report issues with the product's stability, performance, or consistency.
evidence: The Insert Graphics feature in V3 does not work. An error is generated every time.
Defective disc, gets get stuck over and over trying to load in the drive...

signals.description: Users explicitly recommend the product to others or express that they would share it.
evidence: My boyfriend liked my app so much, he got it for his iPhone.
Best tax product available in my view.

signals.description: Users explicitly warn others against buying the product or share negative experiences to deter others.
evidence: Buyers beware!
Don't waste your money.
Windows 8 is a complete disaster and shows what utter contempt Microsoft has for its customers.

signals.description: Users demonstrate technical knowledge by describing specific tests, configurations, or troubleshooting steps.
evidence: After installing and testing on two different W2K system and getting the same results
I tried doing it from inside the app, from the application in system settings, and from the website.

signals.description: Users praise the visual design, graphics, or overall look and feel of the product.
evidence: Excellent graphics! Beautiful selections
Excellent graphics! Beautiful selections

signals.description: Users indicate that price increases or hidden fees are a significant barrier or point of dissatisfaction.
evidence: The price has doubled though since I first started using this product and as a senior on a fixed income that is what I don't like.
The pricing is a scam, after you purchase you find state, that you thought was included, actually has a fee to e-file.

signals.description: Users recognize that the product has changed over time, referencing older versions or names.
evidence: Been using it for years, even back when it was MacIntax.
Been using it for years, even back when it was MacIntax.

signals.description: Users specifically mention enjoying the ability to adjust settings or combine features to suit their needs.
evidence: Love how easy it is to adjust the settings.
The features I get with premium are perfect for creating the combination of noises that work for me

signals.description: Users describe how frequently or deeply they use the product, indicating integration into routines.
evidence: I use this app nightly.
Been using it for years

signals.description: Users compare their experience across different platforms or devices, noting inconsistencies.
evidence: He has version 5.1 and I have 3.1.
He got a page of sounds that I didn't.

signals.description: Users express that the product's quality or performance has worsened compared to past versions.
evidence: Well, I'll start by saying that this product used to be one of the best, but it appears to have fallen short in 2009.
The price has doubled though since I first started using this product

signals.description: Users express that the product's quality or performance has improved compared to past versions.
Users express that the product's quality or performance has remained consistently good over time.
evidence: Been using it for years, even back when it was MacIntax. Best tax product available in my view.

signals.description: Users express that the product's quality or performance is inconsistent or varies between versions/platforms.
evidence: He has version 5.1 and I have 3.1.
He got a page of sounds that I didn't.

signals.description: Users express uncertainty about the product's quality or performance.
Users express other perceptions about the product's quality or performance.

pain_points.signal: Difficulty finding accessible, human customer support (no phone number, requires searching and emailing).
Slow computer performance after installing new software versions.
Frustration with installation errors and time-consuming setup.
Software updates that are not smooth or require multiple restarts.
Lack of customer loyalty discounts for long-term users.

desired_outcomes.outcome: Achieve product-market fit and understand why early users pay.
Get actionable feedback from users to guide product development.
Seamless, easy installation and uninstallation without errors or extra steps.
Software that works reliably almost all the time without slowing the system.
Automated background processes (updates, scans) requiring no manual oversight.
Clear path from first paying customers to scaling to 100 users.
Software that is simple, straightforward, and trustworthy.
Effective and affordable protection for multiple devices.

objections.objection: App is missing core functionality compared to web

switching_triggers.reason: Degrading user experience after auto-update

pain_points.signal: Bugs and instability, especially on Android, that persist or resurface after fixes.
Frustration with forced UI changes, like intrusive pop-ups or non-savable sort defaults.
Ineffective or buggy mobile moderation tools requiring desktop for admin tasks.
Unreliable login and authentication flow causing lockouts and expired links.
Poor mobile/tablet UI with wasted screen space and non-native assumptions.
Buggy platform-specific features (Android auto-answer, Google Buds audio routing).
Disruptive UX during reading due to UI elements like 'is writing' bar.
Bad search functionality returning irrelevant results.
Missing basic features like easy sign-out or email switching.

desired_outcomes.outcome: Stable, bug-free core functionality, especially for mobile platforms.
Streamlined, intuitive interface without unnecessary features or clutter.
Full-featured mobile admin capabilities for moderation and member management.
Reliable notification system for calls (huddles) without phantom UI.
Painless, reliable login and device/workspace sync process.
Predictable, customizable UI with savable settings (filters, sort, common emojis).
Effective search and easy file/attachment handling.

switching_triggers.reason: Persistent bugs and regressions make core functionality untrustworthy.
